sector.name: MFI
sector.description: Microfinance lenders recovering with tighter guardrails

fyPerformance: AUM stood at ₹4,420 Cr, up 12% QoQ, increasing after 8 quarters
Q4FY26 monthly disbursement run-rate stood around ₹500 Cr vs ₹400 Cr in Q3FY26 and ₹300 Cr in Q2FY26
Q4FY26 PAT stood at ₹5 Cr vs loss of ₹95 Cr in Q3FY26
Q4FY26 PBT stood at ₹8 Cr vs loss of ₹125 Cr in Q3FY26; PPOP stood at ₹39 Cr vs ₹8 Cr in Q3FY26
Yield stood at 22.8% vs 22.4% in Q3FY26; NIM stood at 9.9%; liquidity stood at ₹1,438 Cr
highlights: New book now forms 80% of AUM and has been originated under the new guardrails / BRE framework
X-bucket collection efficiency stood at 99.7%, with new book showing 99.7% collection efficiency over 8 months of seasoning
Customer base stood at 11.5 lakh+, and company added around 1.2 lakh new borrowers in Q4FY26
1-90 DPD AUM improved to 1.3% vs 2.5% QoQ; GNPA improved to 3.8% vs 4.2% QoQ
Provision coverage remains above 80%; company collected nearly ₹65 Cr from 90+ DPD customers for the second consecutive quarter
Individual loan product is expected before the end of the quarter or early next quarter
Spandana Sphoorty Financial Ltd.quote: "Spandana is showing a sharp recovery, but it is still in rebuild mode compared to stronger peers like CreditAccess and Satin."

fyPerformance: Consolidated AUM stood at ₹15,174 Cr (+19% YoY); total revenue stood at ₹3,161 Cr (+23% YoY)
PPOP stood at ₹928 Cr (+23% YoY); consolidated PAT stood at ₹330 Cr (+79% YoY)
Q4FY26 PAT grew 640% YoY and 125% QoQ
Standalone AUM stood at ₹12,853 Cr (+14% YoY); standalone disbursement stood at ₹11,202 Cr
Consolidated NIM stood at 13.2%; ROA improved to 2.6% and ROE improved to 12.3%

guidance.value: Stable or improving if repo rates fall

highlights: Management believes the sector had passed its stress peak by early 2026
SRO guardrails, 3 lender cap and ₹2 lakh borrower exposure ceiling have reshaped lending discipline
Standalone GNPA stood at 3.1%; X-bucket collection efficiency stood at 99.9%
Stage 3 coverage improved to 73% vs 67% in December 2025
FY26 credit cost stood at 3.8% vs 4.6% in FY25; Q4FY26 credit cost improved further to 2.5%
Non-MFI businesses now contribute 17% of consolidated AUM
Satin Creditcare Network Ltd.quote: "Satin is outperforming the sector on asset quality, Q4 profitability, credit cost moderation, geographic spread and diversification beyond core MFI."

fyPerformance: Consolidated AUM stood at ₹2,728 Cr (+22% YoY)
Highest-ever quarterly disbursement stood at ₹951 Cr
Q4FY26 consolidated PAT stood at ₹41 Cr, up 85% QoQ and 220% YoY
FY26 PAT stood at ₹57 Cr (+9% YoY)
MFI AUM stood at ₹1,999 Cr (+19% YoY); Q4FY26 MFI disbursement stood at ₹738 Cr (+88% YoY and +62% QoQ)

guidance.value: May get close to peak profitability

highlights: Management said the MFI industry moved beyond the most difficult phase and is entering a stable, disciplined growth cycle
Company separated credit and recovery functions from branch operations, improving accountability and collection efficiency
Around 90% of the MFI portfolio is covered under CGFMU
Company moved away from pure group-based JLG credit assessment towards individual-level borrower assessment
GNPA stood at 3.4% and NNPA stood at 0.95%; X-bucket collection was above 99.5% for Q4FY26
MSME and LAP initiatives are expected to contribute over the next 2-4 quarters
Arman Financial Services Ltd.quote: "Arman is outperforming on Q4 recovery, underwriting discipline and CGFMU coverage, but operating cost remains the biggest monitorable."

fyPerformance: AUM grew 14% YoY and 11% QoQ
Q4FY26 disbursement stood at ₹8,313 Cr (+28% YoY and +44% QoQ); FY26 disbursement stood at ₹24,859 Cr (+24% YoY)
Q4FY26 borrower addition stood at 3.3 lakh; FY26 borrower addition stood at 9.8 lakh
Q4FY26 PPOP stood at ₹780 Cr (+23% YoY and +15% QoQ); Q4FY26 PAT stood at ₹340 Cr, up 6x YoY and 35% QoQ
FY26 PPOP stood at ₹2,809 Cr (+7% YoY); FY26 PAT stood at ₹778 Cr
highlights: Management called Q4FY26 a decisive inflection in performance trajectory
Growth was driven by new-to-credit customers, guardrail-compliant borrowers and graduation of vintage borrowers into retail finance products
Retail finance share increased to 18.1% vs 5.9% last year
Branch network stood at 2,236 branches after adding 183 branches
Gross NPA stood at 3.17%, Net NPA stood at 1.12% and PAR 90 stood at 2.28%
Grameen Mahi app onboarded 8.4 lakh borrowers in FY26; active app base reached 11.2 lakh customers
CreditAccess Grameen Ltd.quote: "CreditAccess is outperforming the sector on scale, profitability, borrower acquisition, digital adoption, cost of borrowing and FY27 return guidance."

fyPerformance: AUM increased to ₹7,400 Cr vs ₹6,800 Cr QoQ
Q4FY26 disbursement rose to ₹2,140 Cr vs ₹1,594 Cr QoQ
Q4FY26 NII stood at ₹222 Cr, NIM stood at 11.4% and PPOP stood at ₹93 Cr
Q4FY26 reported PAT stood at ₹114 Cr; core PAT excluding one-time DTA impact stood at ₹38 Cr
FY26 PAT stood at ₹14 Cr vs loss of around ₹1,200 Cr in FY25
highlights: FY26 was a year of learning, testing and institutional building
Nearly 90% of disbursements now come from A and B category branches
Current bucket forward flow remains below 0.1% on net basis
MFI collection efficiency stood at 99.7%, while MSME collection efficiency was above 99.3%
GNPA improved for the 4th consecutive quarter to 3.21%, while NNPA improved to 0.51%
Fusion raised around ₹6,000 Cr borrowings in FY26 and onboarded 11 new lenders
Fusion Finance Ltd.quote: "Fusion has moved from stress phase to recovery phase, but needs 2-3 more quarters of consistent delivery to prove the turnaround is sustainable."

fyPerformance: Portfolio under management stood at ₹14,005 Cr (+13% YoY); Q4FY26 portfolio growth stood at 7% QoQ
Q4FY26 disbursement stood at ₹2,876 Cr (+46% YoY and +15% QoQ)
Q4FY26 profit stood at ₹71 Cr, highest in the last 7 quarters; FY26 profit stood at ₹170 Cr
Q4FY26 NIM stood at 12%; Q4FY26 opex stood at 6.4% vs peak of around 7% earlier in FY26
FY26 credit cost stood at 3.5% vs 9.4% last year; Q4FY26 ROA stood at 2.1% and ROE stood at 10%
highlights: Management said FY26 saw a return of normal business momentum
Asset quality improved through the year, with GNPA improving to 3.89% vs 4.85% last year
X-bucket collection efficiency stood around 99.8% in the last 2 quarters
Small and micro-enterprise individual loan portfolio stood around ₹2,387 Cr with near-zero delinquency
Non-JLG portfolio already stands around 17% of AUM
Customer app has nearly 2 mn customers out of 3.3 mn customers, and digital collection stands around 40% incrementally
Muthoot Microfin Ltd.quote: "Muthoot Microfin is outperforming on diversification, Q4 recovery, technology-led underwriting and long-term Vision 3030 roadmap."
